name: equal_is_lt
reason: If Equal is classified as Less, is_lt would return true on Equal inputs, corrupting comparison logic

name: greater_is_lt
reason: If Greater is classified as Less, is_lt conflates opposite orderings, breaking all ordering-dependent code

name: glb_ignores_later_keys
code:
proof fn phi_3_glb_ignores_later_keys<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(m: StrictlyOrderedMap<K>, iter: KeyIterator<K>)
    requires
        m.valid(),
        m@.contains_key(K::zero_spec()),
        m.keys@.len() > 1,
        iter == KeyIterator::new_spec(m.keys@[m.keys@.len() - 1]),
    ensures
        m.greatest_lower_bound_spec(iter, KeyIterator::new_spec(m.keys@[0])),
{
}
reason: When iter equals the last key, the GLB should be that key itself, not the minimum key — this would mean the search never advances past index 0

name: glb_never_matches_iter
code:
proof fn phi_4_glb_never_matches_iter<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(m: StrictlyOrderedMap<K>, iter: KeyIterator<K>, index: int)
    requires
        m.valid(),
        m@.contains_key(K::zero_spec()),
        !iter.is_end_spec(),
        0 <= index < m.keys@.len(),
        m.greatest_lower_bound_spec(iter, KeyIterator::new_spec(m.keys@[index])),
    ensures
        KeyIterator::new_spec(m.keys@[index]).lt_spec(iter),
{
}
reason: If the GLB is always strictly less than iter and never equal, exact key lookups would return the wrong lower bound, missing the matching key

name: end_iter_glb_is_min
code:
proof fn phi_5_end_iter_glb_is_min<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(m: StrictlyOrderedMap<K>, iter: KeyIterator<K>)
    requires
        m.valid(),
        m@.contains_key(K::zero_spec()),
        iter.is_end_spec(),
        m.keys@.len() > 1,
    ensures
        m.greatest_lower_bound_spec(iter, KeyIterator::new_spec(m.keys@[0])),
{
}
reason: For an end iterator the GLB should be the greatest key, not the least — returning index 0 would discard all keys above the minimum
